name: erdos530-mathlib-finitetype-cache-control
hypothesis: The pinned Mathlib FiniteType OLean is independently readable; if this small import passes, the umbrella-target failure is aggregate resource pressure rather than a missing or corrupt cache file.
expected_signal: The exact import exits zero and prints the checked declaration; otherwise retain the first cache compatibility or read error.
decision_value: Separates a corrupt cache artifact from aggregate FormalConjecturesUtil import pressure before any further target retry.

efficiency_design.naive_cost: Rehydrate and rebuild a second 6.9 GiB Mathlib tree, then invoke a full repository build before learning whether the one numeric module target elaborates.
opportunities_considered: Reuse the manifest-identical pinned Mathlib package and compiled-object cache instead of cloning or hydrating it again.
Build only the disambiguated numeric module target before any full-repository validation.
Use one Lean thread and a warning-fatal invocation under the checkpointed resource-limited lab.
Hash the driver and candidate as immutable lab inputs and record checkout, candidate, manifest, toolchain, stdout, and stderr hashes.
Retain the prior finite semantic controls rather than rerunning an arbitrary larger cutoff.
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Share only the manifest-identical package cache, keep workspace build outputs isolated, and run one exact +FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.«530» target with --wfail.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: Avoids a second multi-GiB download and turns the discriminator into one bounded target build; expected wall time is minutes rather than a full hydrate plus full build.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: Both checkouts have identical lake-manifest SHA-256, Mathlib commit, and Lean 4.27 toolchain. Lake and Lean kernel perform the target check; warning-fatal mode rejects warnings; durable lab records retain exact inputs and outputs.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: The target module and its transitive local Formal Conjectures support imports still compile normally. A later full repository build, repository CI, and human semantic review remain separate required gates.
